---
title: "Methods"
---

This is a **rapid living review** following the Cochrane Rapid Reviews
Methods Group (Garritty et al. 2021) and reported per **PRISMA-RR**
(Stevens et al. 2024). Pre-registered on PROSPERO. Full protocol:
`protocol/PRISMA-P_protocol.md`.

## Eligibility

Adults (≥ 18 years) with atopic dermatitis defined by Hanifin-Rajka, UK
Working Party criteria, ICD-10 L20, or validated self-report; vs adults
without AD. Outcomes: depression, anxiety, suicidality / self-harm —
pooled separately.

## Searches

PubMed and Embase queries (`search/pubmed_query.txt`,
`search/embase_query.txt`) re-run monthly via GitHub Actions. Cochrane
CENTRAL and reference-list hand-searches of all included studies.
PsycINFO and grey literature limited to the top three most-cited reviews
(rapid-review streamline). PRESS-2015 peer review on file.

## Rapid-review streamlining

| Step | This review | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|
| Title/abstract screen | One reviewer | 20% re-screened by same reviewer after ≥ 14-day washout; intra-rater κ reported |
| Full text | One reviewer | 10% re-screened after washout |
| Extraction | One reviewer | Self-verification against PDF for high-risk items after ≥ 7-day washout |
| Risk of bias | One reviewer (ROBINS-E / RoB 2) | 20% re-rated after washout |

Calibration: 50-record pilot screened twice by the reviewer with ≥ 14-day
washout; intra-rater κ ≥ 0.8 required to begin formal screening.

## Quantitative synthesis

- Random-effects with REML τ²; Hartung-Knapp-Sidik-Jonkman CIs.
- Effect harmonised to OR; HR/RR-only studies sensitivity-tested.
- Heterogeneity via τ², I², 95% prediction interval.
- Pre-specified subgroups: age stratum, AD severity, sex, region,
  ascertainment, study design.
- Meta-regression on mean age and % female (k ≥ 10).
- Sensitivity: leave-one-out, low-RoB-only, adjusted-only, incident-only,
  OR-native-only.
- Publication bias: funnel plot + Egger (k ≥ 10) + trim-and-fill.

## Certainty

GRADE per outcome. The mechanical draft contributes **one downgrade for
the rapid-review single-reviewer design** unless intra-rater validation
κ ≥ 0.8, in which case the downgrade is dropped (Garritty 2024 GRADE-rapid).
